events.text: Kevin Thompson, 45-year-old White male, Former smoker (quit 10 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.

Diagnosed with Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the Base of tongue. Clinical staging: T3N1M0, AJCC Stage III. ICD-10: C10.9. Stage III HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx.
PET/CT performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 dermatitis. Grade 2 radiation dermatitis. Aquaphor and silver sulfadiazine cream applied.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated definitive concurrent chemoradiation: Cisplatin 100 mg/m2 q3w x 3 cycles concurrent with radiation. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 xerostomia. Pilocarpine 5 mg TID initiated. Artificial saliva recommended.
MRI neck with contrast performed for restaging after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
Clinical visit for results review after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.
Initiated bioradiation: Cetuximab 400 mg/m2 loading then 250 mg/m2 weekly x 7 concurrent with radiation. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated bioradiation: Cetuximab 400 mg/m2 loading then 250 mg/m2 weekly x 7 concurrent with radiation. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 xerostomia. Pilocarpine 5 mg TID initiated. Artificial saliva recommended.
Initiated bioradiation: Cetuximab 400 mg/m2 loading then 250 mg/m2 weekly x 7 concurrent with radiation. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 xerostomia. Pilocarpine 5 mg TID initiated. Artificial saliva recommended.
PET/CT performed for restaging after bioradiation.
Clinical visit for results review after bioradiation.
CT neck with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI neck with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI neck with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Kevin Thompson
MRN: patient_3af9e3999cdd
Date of Study: 2022-03-24

CLINICAL INDICATION: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: No pelvic mass or lymphadenopathy. Bladder unremarkable.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Oropharyngeal mass with cervical lymphadenopathy. Clinical staging consistent with Stage III.

Electronically signed by: Robert Chang, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Kevin Thompson
MRN: patient_3af9e3999cdd
Specimen ID: S2022-55315
Date of Procedure: 2022-03-25
Date of Report: 2022-03-28

SPECIMEN: Base of tongue, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated), poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.8 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 1.2 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Present

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
p16: Positive (strong, diffuse)
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive
EBV: Negative

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Kevin Thompson - Base of tongue" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 6.8 x 4.8 x 3.4 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of sheets of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Kevin Thompson
MRN: patient_3af9e3999cdd
Date: 2022-04-03
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Thompson is a 45-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue, III. Diagnosis made following evaluation of progressive symptoms. Stage III HPV-positive squamous cell carcinoma of the oropharynx. Pathology confirmed squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated). Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports abdominal discomfort and early satiety.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obesity

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Cesarean section x 2 (if applicable)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Former smoker (quit 5 years ago)
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Construction worker (retired)
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No significant family history.

ALLERGIES: Sulfa drugs (hives)

MEDICATIONS:
- None

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: night sweats, mild dyspnea on exertion. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 123/72, HR 75, RR 15, Temp 98.5F, SpO2 95% on RA, Wt 82.4 kg
General: Comfortable, thin-appearing, no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: No thyromegaly. No lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, mildly tender in RUQ. No rebound. Normoactive bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. No cyanosis or clubbing.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.4, Hgb 9.7, Plt 154, Cr 0.72

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
45-year-old male with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue. initial oncology consultation.

1. Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue, Stage III
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend definitive concurrent chemoradiation
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Port placement scheduled
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Carlos Mendez, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Kevin Thompson
MRN: patient_3af9e3999cdd
Date of Report: 2022-04-11
Specimen: Base of tongue (Resection)
Diagnosis: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated)
Panel: Tempus xT (523 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 8.1 mutations/Mb (Low)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  HPV HPV-16 positive (p16 IHC positive) - Viral [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  PIK3CA p.E545K - Missense (Exon 9)
  NOTCH1 p.P2164fs - Frameshift (Exon 34)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  CASP8 p.R127* - VUS
  FAT1 p.G4551S - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - HPV-positive disease: Generally favorable prognosis. De-escalation trials may be appropriate.
  - PIK3CA mutation: Alpelisib under investigation in HNSCC.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 691x coverage.
Tumor content: 52%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

Date: 2022-05-12
Provider: Dr. Richard Okafor

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Thompson is a 45-year-old male with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue who presents for cycle 2 assessment. Patient reports mild fatigue but otherwise doing well. Reports mild nausea but eating adequately.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: mild dyspnea on exertion, constipation, night sweats, insomnia. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 132/66, HR 76, RR 16, Temp 97.8F, SpO2 96% on RA, Wt 68.4 kg
General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: A&O x3, CN II-XII intact, no focal deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 3.1, Hgb 12.3, Plt 299, Cr 0.99

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
45-year-old male with III squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (hpv-associated) of the base of tongue. cycle 2 assessment.

1. Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) - cycle 2 assessment
   - Treatment well tolerated, continue
   - Tumor markers trending favorably
2. Symptom management
   - Pain well controlled on current regimen
3. Follow-up in 2 weeks

Electronically signed by: Helen Park, MD
Medical Oncology

Patient: Kevin Thompson

Date of Study: 2022-06-29

CLINICAL INDICATION: Squamous cell carcinoma, p16-positive (HPV-associated) of the base of tongue. restaging after definitive concurrent chemoradiation.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
OROPHARYNX: Enhancing mass centered in the base of tongue measuring 3.6 x 2.5 cm with extension to the ipsilateral tonsil.
NECK: Level IIB lymphadenopathy on the right, largest node 2.3 cm with heterogeneous enhancement.

IMPRESSION:
1. Oropharyngeal mass with cervical lymphadenopathy. Clinical staging consistent with Stage III.
